name: Nicotinamide enhanced UV DNA-damage repair in human keratinocytes and ex vivo skin
summary: Mechanistic human-cell and ex vivo skin evidence supports nicotinamide as a plausible DNA-repair enhancer in UV-damaged skin.
statement: In human keratinocytes and ex vivo human skin, nicotinamide increased repair of UV-induced DNA damage and reduced CPD and 8-oxoG markers.
population_or_model: Human keratinocytes and ex vivo human skin
caveats: This is not an in vivo clinical trial.
The model is UV-damaged skin, not broad aging-related genome maintenance across tissues.
Clinical benefit has to be inferred from separate studies.
